rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may put a basic Energy card from their discard pile into their hand.

During this turn, your Fusion Strike Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

flavorText: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

rules:
Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards, Special Energy cards, and Stadium cards from it.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 1, 3, or 5 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose up to 2 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fusion Strike Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If you go second and it's your first turn, draw 3 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 3 Fusion Strike Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

This card can only be attached to a Fusion Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Fusion Strike Pokémon, discard this card. As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to the Pokémon this card is attached to.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Water Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent's strength.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Jumping nimbly about, this small-bodied Pokémon takes advantage of even the slightest opportunity to disorient larger opponents.

attacks.name: Turn a Profit
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Gimme Gimme
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flying Stomp
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It's very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight's flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.
It's notably wary and has a dislike of fighting, but at the same time, it can deliver powerful kicks with its lithe legs.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The reason it keeps one ear rolled up is so it can launch a swift counterattack if it's attacked by an enemy.

attacks.name: Willful Busybody
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

attacks.name: Sketching Trash
attacks.effect: Put up to 2 Fusion Strike Trainer cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

attacks.name: Wild Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.
When it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.
This Pokémon's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

attacks.name: Continuous Steps
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

attacks.name: Coordinated One-Two Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: You shouldn't get close to the child when it's playing outside its mother's pouch. Its mother is always nearby watching over it.

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon is overflowing with love. Only Chansey that share a strong bond with their Trainer can evolve, so people say.

attacks.name: Drain Slap
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Because the eggs on their bellies have been overharvested by people in the past, the Chansey population remains very small.

attacks.name: No Reprieve
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rampage Drill
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its elegant and refined behavior clashes with that of the barbaric Perrserker. The relationship between the two is one of mutual disdain.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It washes its face regularly to keep the coin on its forehead spotless. It doesn't get along with Galarian Meowth.

attacks.name: Shell Trap
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.
Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The lump on its back contains its tiny brain. It thinks only of food and escaping its enemies.
Their horns are powerful sensors. As soon as Goomy pick up any sign of enemies, they go into hiding. This is how they've survived.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

attacks.name: Dyna Barrier
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region's airborne taxi service.

attacks.name: Ingot Swing
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon that have an Ability.

attacks.name: Blasting Hammer
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

attacks.name: Iron Intake
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

attacks.name: Unlock
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Long ago it lived in mines, but once the minerals that make up its diet became scarcer, Klefki began appearing in human settlements.

attacks.name: Techno Blast
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Adversity Jaws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fire Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

attacks.name: Rock Tomb
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its body is draped in steel armor. It looks heavy, but it can fly at speeds of up to 185 miles an hour!

attacks.name: Call to Muster
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: These daring Pokémon have coins on their foreheads. Darker coins are harder, and harder coins garner more respect among Meowth.

attacks.name: Explosive Discontent
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

attacks.name: Rear Attack
attacks.effect: If you have 2 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.

flavorText: Its hairs work like muscle fibers. When its hairs unfurl, they latch on to opponents, ensnaring them as tentacles would.

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: With sly cunning, it tries to lure people into the woods. Some believe it to have the power to make crops grow.

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It sneaks into people's homes, stealing things and feasting on the negative energy of the frustrated occupants.
When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

attacks.name: Knocking Hammer
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.

attacks.name: Bone Block
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, Pokémon can't be played from your opponent's hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They adorn themselves with bones. There seem to be fashion trends among them, as different bones come into and fall out of popularity.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Vullaby grow quickly. Bones that have gotten too small for older Vullaby to wear often get passed down to younger ones in the nest.

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.
If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.
It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 4 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: It booms out a victory croak when its prey goes down in defeat. This Pokémon and Seismitoad are related species.
Once diluted, its poison becomes medicinal. This Pokémon came into popularity after a pharmaceutical company chose it as a mascot.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The elderly call it the disaster Pokémon and detest it, but interest in its power to predict disasters is on the rise.
It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It's very sensitive to the scent of blood.
With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.

attacks.name: Silence
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Merciless Strike
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.
This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fear and Panic
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX in play.

attacks.name: G-Max Swallow Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Slumber
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Pain Explosion
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

attacks.name: Cliff Edge Formation
attacks.effect: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 160 more damage.

flavorText: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.
Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.
Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn't alarmed when that happens—it knows they'll grow back. It's about as smart as a three-year-old.

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.
The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

attacks.name: Raised Bad
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Darkness Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Wanting to make sure it's taken seriously, Pancham's always giving others a glare. But if it's not focusing, it ends up smiling.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthen Boom
attacks.effect: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.
It's a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ancient Imprint
attacks.effect: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 60.

flavorText: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.
It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.
Hariyama that are big and fat aren't necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.
There's a rumor of a traditional recipe for stew that Trainers can use to raise strong Makuhita.

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Venomous Hit
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

attacks.name: Powerful Rage
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It detonates its own body. The power from that explosion can propel it up steep mountain paths with amazing speed.
It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.
It uses both hands to climb precipitous cliffs. People who see it in action have been known to take up bouldering.

attacks.name: Gut Punch
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The blood vessels in its brain are sturdier than those of other Pokémon, so it can stay healthy despite its constant raging.
If one gets angry, all the others around it will get angry, so silence is a rare visitor in a troop of Mankey.

attacks.name: Dig Uppercut
attacks.effect: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Thanks to its thick claws, it's good at climbing trees. There are plenty of Sandslash that park themselves in trees and go right to sleep.

attacks.name: Dig It Up
attacks.effect: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: When its skin gets wrinkled from moisture, it heads for a volcano. It lies flat on a spot with a lot of geothermal heat and dries itself out.

attacks.name: Fusion Strike Assault
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Apparently the Dreepy inside Dragapult's horns eagerly look forward to being launched out at Mach speeds.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Without a Dreepy to place on its head and care for, it gets so uneasy it'll try to substitute any Pokémon it finds for the missing Dreepy.

attacks.name: Infestation
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: If this weak Pokémon is by itself, a mere child could defeat it. But if Dreepy has friends to help it train, it can evolve and become much stronger.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They diligently serve people and Pokémon so they can gather feelings of gratitude. The females are particularly good at babysitting.

attacks.name: Oppressing Sandstorm
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Once it has whipped up a sandstorm to halt its opponents in their tracks, this terrifying Pokémon snatches away their vitality.
If you build sand mounds when you're playing, destroy them before you go home, or they may get possessed and become Sandygast.

attacks.name: Melodious Echo
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage for each Fusion Strike Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A discovery was made in the desert where Sigilyph fly. The ruins of what may have been an ancient city were found beneath the sands.

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It drowses and dreams all the time. It's best to leave it be if it's just woken up, as it's a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.
It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it's eating a good dream, and black when it's eating a nightmare.

attacks.name: Photon Boost
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fusion Strike Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

subtype: Basic, Fusion Strike, Single Strike, Rapid Strike
flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

attacks.name: Chomp Chomp Hold
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

attacks.name: Force Regeneration
attacks.effect: Put a Basic Pokémon V from your opponent's discard pile onto their Bench. If you do, put damage counters on that Pokémon until its remaining HP is 30.

flavorText: Be cautious of the ectoplasmic body surrounding its soul. You'll become stiff as stone if you touch it.
Sudden climate change wiped out this ancient kind of Corsola. This Pokémon absorbs others' life-force through its branches.
Although it's popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it's totally incompetent as a watchdog.
In contrast to its appearance, it's quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

attacks.name: Cross Fusion Strike
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Max Miracle
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Mix
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Leap
attacks.effect: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

attacks.name: Find Treasure
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it gets angry, it inhales with all its might, and its body gradually inflates. Sometimes they can grow 20 times larger!

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: The songs they sing are totally different depending on the region they live in. Some even sound like they're shouting!

attacks.name: Targeted Spark
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It carries electrically roasted seeds with it as if they're precious treasures. No matter how much it eats, it always gets hungry again in short order.
It has an electrical organ on its chest. While generating electricity, it fills its surroundings with sounds like the strumming of a bass guitar.

attacks.name: Punk Shock
attacks.effect: You may choose to make your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed. If you do, this Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

flavorText: This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.
It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

attacks.name: Bolt Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Bolt
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Max Bolt.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

attacks.name: Electro Blaster
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 200 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If it carries a Charjabug to use as a spare battery, a flying Vikavolt can rapidly fire high-powered beams of electricity.
Its digestive processes convert the leaves it eats into electricity. An electric sac in its belly stores the electricity for later use.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.
The sun powers this Pokémon's electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

attacks.name: Upper Shock
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved from Eelektrik during this turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

attacks.name: Surprise Short
attacks.effect: Discard all Pokémon Tools from all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: With a body made of plasma, it can inhabit all sorts of machines. It loves to surprise others.
Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.
Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.
All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

attacks.name: Spark Duo
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Minun used an attack during your last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

attacks.name: Sonic Boom
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

attacks.name: Single Shot Blast
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Blizzard Loop
attacks.effect: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

attacks.name: Find Ice
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.
It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.
After it's eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That's when Cramorant swallows it up.

attacks.name: Hard Bite
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.
It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

attacks.name: G-Max Spiral
attacks.effect: You may put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It's covered in a slime that keeps its skin moist, allowing it to stay on land for days without drying up.

attacks.name: Trigger Avalanche
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Destroyer Punch
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Snipe Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its right arm is packed with meat. When its pincer falls off, it's exported to be used as a cooking ingredient.
It moves around by jetting water from its right pincer. It has a poor sense of balance, so it's terrible at swimming straight.

attacks.name: Dancing Shuriken
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Daruma Headbutt
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for Water.

flavorText: Though it has a gentle disposition, it's also very strong. It will quickly freeze the snowball on its head before going for a headbutt.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The colder they get, the more energetic they are. They freeze their breath to make snowballs, using them as ammo for playful snowball fights.

attacks.name: Swarm the Wound
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Known for their violence, these Pokémon have the most fights with schools of red-striped Basculin.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Circus Soaking
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Supporter card you find there.

flavorText: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

attacks.name: Pry
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.
Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.
Clamperl's pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder's pearls.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.effect: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Living on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.
To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: As it majestically swims, it doesn't care if Remoraid attach to it to scavenge for its leftovers.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: The small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.

attacks.name: Dive and Rescue
attacks.effect: Put up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it's in the water.

attacks.name: Aqua Liner
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Let's All Rollout
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let's All Rollout attack.

flavorText: Even after Marill swims in a cold sea, its water- repellent fur dries almost as soon as Marill leaves the water. That's why this Pokémon is never cold.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.
Once it bites down, it won't let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.
Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its Trainer must be careful.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Splash Arch
attacks.effect: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

attacks.name: Multishot Star
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of Water Energy from this Pokémon. Then, for each Energy card you discarded in this way, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and do 30 damage to it. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It rotates its geometrically shaped body to swim through the water. It always seems to be sending out mysterious radio waves.

attacks.name: Soak in Water
attacks.effect: Attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: No number of injuries can bother Staryu. Its amazing regenerative powers return it to its previous state in half a day!

attacks.name: Aqua Split
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Once it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superior strength.
Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 90 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.
It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

attacks.name: G-Max Fireball
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: All-Out Shot
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blaze Kick
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Glistening Droplets
attacks.effect: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.effect: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Exciting Flame
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack also does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

attacks.name: Max Poltergeist
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 70 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 40 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fling Fire
attacks.effect: Discard up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Fiery Cheering
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

attacks.name: Body Splash
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.
Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A Pokémon that has long been admired for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.

attacks.name: Warm Up
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Extremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own Trainer from harm.
Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.
Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.
As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.
As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

attacks.name: Wild Whip
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Once the vines on Zarude's body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

attacks.name: Sweet Impact
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sunny Wind
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.
It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

attacks.name: G-Max Drum Solo
attacks.effect: This attack also does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Drain Punch
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Queen's Orders
attacks.effect: You may discard any number of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bubble Launch
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.
Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.effect: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.
Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon's feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.
With a voice like a human child's, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

attacks.name: Bail Out
attacks.effect: Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Legends say this Pokémon confounded opponents with its swift movements.

attacks.name: Ninja Tornado
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack can be used for Grass.

flavorText: Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane.
It has a strange physiology that responds to electricity. When together with Karrablast, Shelmet evolves for some reason.

attacks.name: Ditch and Shake
attacks.effect: Discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They're jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

attacks.name: Healing Circle
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from each of your Benched Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests.

attacks.name: Grass Munch
attacks.effect: Discard a Grass Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

attacks.name: Return
attacks.effect: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.
It's good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It's kind enough to share them with friends.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Impact Blow
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Impact Blow.

flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.
It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.
It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.
Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can't withstand a harsh attack.

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Caterpie and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.
